description: Decide and record one canonical upstream source per theme (repo/file/ref/variant), plus normalization policy into internal palette keys. Clarify when Omarchy is canonical vs when original theme upstream is canonical.
notes:
Context captured on 2026-03-03 (America/Montreal):

Recommended canonical source per current theme:
- tokyo-night: folke/tokyonight.nvim | variant=night | reference file=extras/ghostty/tokyonight_night
- gruvbox: morhetz/gruvbox | variant=dark-hard | reference file=colors/gruvbox.vim
- everforest: sainnhe/everforest | variant=dark-medium | reference files=palette.md and autoload/everforest.vim
- osaka-jade: basecamp/omarchy | variant=dark | reference file=themes/osaka-jade/colors.toml

Policy recommendation:
- Use per-theme upstream canonical source (not Omarchy-for-all)
- Pin each theme source to repo+file+ref(tag/commit)+variant
- Normalize into internal palette keys, then generate all app artifacts
- Use overrides only for app-specific deviations

Key findings:
- Omarchy does not currently expose a per-theme canonical upstream mapping field in themes metadata/docs; treat Omarchy as canonical set itself unless theme-specific upstream is chosen.
- Local drift spotted in current repo:
  - themes/themes.json tokyo-night has several accent values not present in current tokyonight upstream main
  - everforest bg mapping differs from canonical medium ordering (bg1/bg2)

Upstream repos verified (HTTP 200 on 2026-03-03):
- https://github.com/folke/tokyonight.nvim
- https://github.com/morhetz/gruvbox
- https://github.com/sainnhe/everforest
- https://github.com/basecamp/omarchy
title: Define canonical upstream sources + variant pinning

close_reason: Implemented a more fault-tolerant AeroSpace on-focus-changed callback and verified reload; documented that Raycast app shortcuts bypass AeroSpace bindings and AutoRaise third-party switcher warping is inherently less reliable.
description: Analyze current dotfiles configuration for AutoRaise, AeroSpace, and Raycast. Determine whether AeroSpace can center the cursor on focus/app switch, identify why cursor warping is unreliable with Raycast app shortcuts, and propose or implement a more reliable fix.
notes: Root cause analysis: Raycast app shortcuts bypass AeroSpace bindings, so any cursor movement tied only to bindings will not run. AutoRaise docs explicitly warn mouse warp is less reliable with third-party task switchers even with altTaskSwitcher=true. Added a more fault-tolerant AeroSpace on-focus-changed callback to home/office/laptop configs using window-lazy-center with monitor-lazy-center fallback so AeroSpace can recenter after external focus changes it observes.
title: Investigate unreliable cursor centering with AutoRaise + AeroSpace + Raycast app switching

description: Current experiments showed AeroSpace on-focus-changed callbacks cannot distinguish manual mouse/hover focus from keyboard-driven app/workspace switches. That makes callback-based cursor centering fundamentally wrong for the desired behavior: center on Raycast/cmd-tab style switches, but never steal the pointer during manual mouse movement. Investigate a source-aware approach instead: likely remove generic AeroSpace focus-centering callbacks, keep logging, and evaluate targeted mechanisms such as AutoRaise alt-task-switcher warp for cmd-tab, Raycast-specific wrapper scripts/hotkeys, or another tool that can identify keyboard/app-switch initiated focus changes. Capture the need to stop workspace 9 -> 1 -> 5 bounce regressions and preserve no-warp behavior for actual mouse movement.
title: Replace generic AeroSpace focus callback with source-aware cursor centering

description:
Goal: Add Chromium browser theming support for the existing theme pipeline, starting with Vantasteez, while preserving the canonical source model.

Current state:
- No Chromium/Chrome integration exists in themes/scripts/theme_build.py or themes/.local/bin/theme-set.
- Canonical source for Vantasteez is themes/sources/vantasteez.toml.
- Generated artifacts currently target meta/, configs/, themes.json, wallpapers/.

Requirements:
1) Keep source-of-truth model: edit only themes/sources/*.toml; generated artifacts are managed output.
2) Implement Chromium theme generation from canonical palette (Vantasteez first, but design should support all themes).
3) Decide and document artifact format (e.g., extension-manifest-based theme package metadata and/or browser-consumable JSON), with rationale.
4) Integrate into generator workflow and tests:
   - Extend themes/scripts/theme_build.py in a non-breaking way.
   - Add/adjust tests in themes/tests/test_theme_build.py.
5) Integrate into runtime apply path if feasible:
   - Update themes/.local/bin/theme-set with a safe Chromium apply step (or document manual apply if fully automated apply is not practical).
6) Update docs:
   - themes/README.md
   - themes/AGENTS.md

Suggested implementation checklist for next session:
- Research/confirm practical Chromium theme apply mechanism on macOS (automated vs manual constraints).
- Add generation target(s) under themes/configs/<theme>/ for Chromium artifacts.
- Wire generator output cleanup for stale files, consistent with existing managed artifacts behavior.
- Add validation and tests for schema/override keys if introducing new overrides.* target.
- Run full validation pipeline:
  python3 themes/scripts/theme_build.py --check
  python3 themes/scripts/theme_build.py --generate-meta
  python3 themes/scripts/theme_build.py --generate-themes-json
  python3 themes/scripts/theme_build.py --generate-configs
  python3 themes/scripts/theme_build.py --generate-wallpapers
  python3 -m unittest themes/tests/test_theme_build.py

Acceptance criteria:
- Chromium artifact(s) generated from canonical palette for Vantasteez.
- No manual edits needed outside canonical source + generators.
- Tests pass and docs describe usage and limitations clearly.
title: Add Chromium theme pipeline for Vantasteez

close_reason: Committed fix f31c7a6: make ghostty no-folding in installer and docs to prevent theme-set writes from dirtying repo.
description: theme-set writes custom Ghostty theme files to ~/.config/ghostty/themes. If ghostty is stowed with folder folding, ~/.config/ghostty points into the repo and theme switching creates untracked files (e.g. ghostty/.config/ghostty/themes/Vantablack). Make ghostty a no-folding package and repair local setup.
title: Prevent Ghostty theme-set from dirtying repo via Stow folding

acceptance_criteria:
- The theme pipeline uses a staged runtime/current-theme model inspired by Omarchy, adapted for macOS apps and workflows.
- Validation of canonical theme sources remains intact.
- Simple app outputs use templates or an equivalent lower-complexity emission path.
- Repo/runtime contracts are updated deliberately, with explicit compatibility decisions for Raycast, installer flows, and state files.
- Obsolete migration artifacts and docs are removed only after the new contract is in place.
description:
Adopt the parts of Omarchy's theme pipeline that make sense on macOS without porting Omarchy wholesale. The target state is an Omarchy-inspired staged runtime model for theme generation and application: lightweight templates for simple outputs, runtime/current-theme staging before activation, preserved validation for canonical sources, and macOS-specific apply/reload logic for SketchyBar, Ghostty, Borders, tmux, Neovim, Obsidian, OpenCode, Antigravity, and wallpapers.

This epic exists because the current theme system is tightly coupled to committed repo artifacts (`themes/configs/*`, `themes/meta/*.env`, `themes/themes.json`) and multiple adjacent consumers assume those artifacts are present. The migration needs to be sequenced so Raycast, `theme-set`, `wallpaper-set`, installer flows, state-file contracts, and user-facing CLI behavior keep working while the internals move toward a staged runtime model.
title: Adopt Omarchy-style staged runtime theme pipeline for macOS

acceptance_criteria:
- A committed decision/ADR defines the macOS target architecture, staged directory layout, discovery/manifest strategy, and compatibility guarantees.
- The document states which generated artifacts stay, move, or disappear.
- The rollout plan is phased so work can land without breaking Raycast, installer, or CLI behavior.
description:
Lock the target architecture before changing implementation details. Decide exactly what we are borrowing from Omarchy and what remains macOS-specific:
- staged runtime directory layout and stable symlink/include targets
- whether `themes/themes.json` remains a repo artifact, becomes a runtime artifact, or is replaced by direct source discovery
- whether `themes/meta/*.env` survives, is replaced, or becomes runtime-only
- which contracts remain stable (`theme-set`, `wallpaper-set`, `~/.config/current-theme`, `~/.config/current-wallpaper`, Ghostty/tmux include paths, Raycast parser expectations)
- migration phases, rollback path, and cutover criteria

This decision should explicitly reject Linux-only Omarchy assumptions that do not apply on macOS.
title: Lock Omarchy-inspired macOS theme architecture and compatibility contract

acceptance_criteria:
- Theme source loading/validation is separated cleanly from output emission.
- Emission supports staging/runtime destinations in addition to legacy repo destinations during migration.
- A unified generate-all orchestration path exists.
- Existing outputs remain reproducible until the cutover is complete.
description:
Refactor `themes/scripts/theme_build.py` so validation/loading is reusable independently from artifact emission. The generator should support both the existing repo outputs during migration and a staged runtime output mode needed by the new `theme-set` flow.

This task is the internal backbone of the migration. It should also eliminate the current four-command regeneration friction by introducing a unified orchestration entrypoint.
title: Refactor theme generator into validation/loading and emission layers

acceptance_criteria:
- Simple config outputs render from templates instead of bespoke Python string builders.
- Output filenames and semantics stay compatible during migration.
- Template parity tests cover the migrated outputs.
description: Reduce generator complexity by moving the simple text outputs to templates or an equivalent declarative render layer, while preserving validation and output parity. Initial targets are the outputs that are mostly token substitution today: SketchyBar, Borders, tmux, Ghostty selector config, and likely the Obsidian CSS snippet.
title: Template simple theme outputs inside the generator

acceptance_criteria:
- Generator can write a complete staged theme payload to a temp/runtime directory.
- Stale managed files are pruned in staged output mode.
- Required runtime metadata/manifest outputs are emitted for `theme-set` consumption.
- A unified generation command can drive the staged output flow.
description: Add a staging/runtime emission mode to the generator. Runtime output must prune stale managed files, emit whatever manifest/metadata bundle `theme-set` consumes, and support a stable current-theme directory instead of assuming committed `themes/configs/*` and `themes/meta/*.env` are the source of truth.
title: Add staged output cleanup and runtime metadata emission

acceptance_criteria:
- Theme source schema and loader support sidecar custom app files where appropriate.
- Precedence rules between palette-derived output and full custom files are documented and enforced.
- The source contract no longer requires heavyweight custom configs to live inside TOML strings.
description: Add a first-class source model for full custom app files so the system no longer relies on large multiline TOML payloads for heavyweight customization. This should preserve canonical-source discipline while making Omarchy-style escape hatches possible for macOS apps that need full-file control.
title: Add sidecar custom app-file support to theme sources

acceptance_criteria:
- `vantasteez` no longer embeds its heavyweight Neovim customization as raw multiline TOML.
- The migrated source path is validated by the generator.
- Generated/runtime output remains functionally equivalent after the migration.
description: Use `vantasteez` as the proving ground for the new custom-file source model by moving the current Neovim customization out of TOML and validating output parity. This is the clearest current case where the TOML-only contract is straining.
title: Migrate Vantasteez Neovim customization out of TOML

acceptance_criteria:
- `theme-set` stages the selected theme before activation.
- Runtime application no longer depends on committed repo config/env artifacts for the active theme.
- Stable include/symlink targets continue to work for tmux, Ghostty, SketchyBar, Borders, Neovim, and optional Ghostty/OpenCode theme files.
- Failure handling and backup behavior are preserved or improved.
description:
Rework `themes/.local/bin/theme-set` around a two-phase flow: generate/stage the selected theme, then activate/apply it. The new flow must preserve the macOS-specific app apply semantics and stable target paths that the rest of the dotfiles assume.

This task should remove the hard dependency on committed `themes/configs/<theme>` and `themes/meta/<theme>.env` at runtime.
title: Implement staged runtime generation and apply flow in theme-set

acceptance_criteria:
- Symlinked app targets resolve through the staged runtime theme directory.
- Existing stable include paths remain valid.
- Optional per-theme Ghostty/OpenCode theme files and Neovim guard behavior still work.
description: Update the symlinkable app targets to point at a stable staged runtime theme directory instead of repo-managed per-theme artifact directories. Preserve existing include path contracts and edge-case behavior like the Neovim Stow-folding guard plus optional Ghostty/OpenCode custom theme files.
title: Preserve stable symlink targets against staged runtime paths

acceptance_criteria:
- In-place writes are as safe and atomic as practical for each target.
- Backup behavior remains intact.
- Permission/failure messages stay clear for macOS users.
- Runtime apply semantics remain compatible with the staged flow.
description: Harden the in-place mutation steps that cannot be handled purely by symlink swapping: Obsidian appearance/snippet updates, Antigravity JSONC edits, OpenCode config writes, wallpaper automation, backup behavior, and user-facing failure handling.
title: Harden in-place app mutations and failure handling during theme apply

acceptance_criteria:

description: Update `wallpaper-set` to work against the staged runtime theme model without breaking current contracts. Preserve `~/.config/current-theme`, `~/.config/current-wallpaper`, and the CLI output format that Raycast currently parses for wallpaper counts.
title: Update wallpaper-set for staged runtime theme state

acceptance_criteria:
- External consumers no longer rely on deprecated `themes/configs/*` discovery assumptions.
- Supported theme discovery and preview contracts are explicit and documented.
- Installer and Raycast flows remain functional after the runtime cutover.
description: Migrate external consumers that currently assume repo-managed theme artifacts. At minimum this covers the Raycast theme switcher/wallpaper commands and the installer's theme discovery flow. The goal is to make theme discovery and previews rely on the supported contract instead of incidental repo layout.
title: Migrate theme discovery consumers to the new runtime contract

acceptance_criteria:

description: Update the Raycast theme switcher and wallpaper commands for the new discovery/runtime contract. Preserve the quality of the preview experience while removing any brittle dependence on deprecated artifact locations or output formats.
title: Update Raycast theme switcher and wallpaper integration

acceptance_criteria:

description: Update `install.sh` so post-install theme selection no longer enumerates `themes/configs/*/` directly. It should use the supported discovery source for available themes and still apply a selected theme correctly on macOS.
title: Update install.sh theme discovery and post-install theme selection

acceptance_criteria:

description: Rewrite the theme-system test suite around staged generation, template parity, runtime compatibility contracts, and cleanup behavior. Existing coverage for strict schema/override validation should be preserved while removing assumptions that every generated artifact must live in git.
title: Rewrite theme tests around staged generation and compatibility contracts

acceptance_criteria:

description: Add focused tests for template-backed outputs and staged emission cleanup so renderer simplification does not silently change user-visible configs.
title: Add template parity and staged output tests

acceptance_criteria:
- Compatibility-critical state/CLI contracts are covered by tests.
- Regressions in theme discovery/apply flow are detectable without manual smoke testing alone.
description: Add focused tests for runtime-facing contracts: selected theme state, wallpaper state, any compatibility-critical CLI output, and the pieces of `theme-set`/`wallpaper-set` behavior that can be validated without full app integration.
title: Add runtime apply contract tests for CLI and state compatibility

acceptance_criteria:

- Old instructions that assume four regeneration commands plus committed artifacts are removed or clearly marked transitional.
- Ownership boundaries between source files, runtime artifacts, and any retained generated artifacts are explicit.
description: Rewrite the theme documentation and agent guidance so it describes the staged runtime model instead of the current committed-artifact workflow. This includes README usage, authoring workflow, ownership boundaries, and troubleshooting.
title: Rewrite theme docs and agent guidance for the staged runtime model

acceptance_criteria:
- README and AGENTS files reflect the new operational model.
- Transitional guidance is clear where backward compatibility still exists.
description: Update `themes/README.md`, `themes/AGENTS.md`, and any root README instructions that currently assume committed generated artifacts are the normal workflow. Replace them with the new staged/current-theme operational model and migration-safe troubleshooting guidance.
title: Update README and AGENTS guidance for the new theme workflow

acceptance_criteria:
- Source schema docs cover sidecar custom files and any new manifest/runtime contract.
- Ownership/migration docs reflect the staged runtime model rather than the old repo-artifact model.
description: Update source-contract docs like `SCHEMA.md` and `MIGRATION_MAPPING.md` so they describe the new source model, sidecar custom-file support, and runtime ownership boundaries accurately.
title: Update schema and ownership docs for the new source/runtime contract

acceptance_criteria:
- Legacy palette/migration leftovers are removed or archived intentionally.
- Repo tree matches the new supported contract.
- Only generated artifacts that still have an explicit purpose remain.
description: After the new contract is in place, remove legacy theme migration artifacts and any redundant generated outputs that are no longer part of the supported workflow. This should include stale migration leftovers like `themes/palettes/*` and historical conductor docs, plus any repo-generated artifacts retired by the architecture decision.
title: Remove legacy theme migration artifacts and obsolete generated outputs

acceptance_criteria:
